// Helper functions (if needed, maybe inside solve or defined globally if scope allows)

def solve(chunks: Array[String], queries: Array[String]): Array[Long] = {
    val S = chunks.mkString("")
    val N = S.length

    // Helper function to check if a character is a lowercase letter
    def isLetter(i: Int): Boolean = {
        val char = S.charAt(i)
        char >= 'a' && char <= 'z'
    }

    // Helper function to check if S[i] is a valid character composing a word
    def isValidChar(i: Int): Boolean = {
        val char = S.charAt(i)

        if (char >= 'a' && char <= 'z') {
            return true
        }

        if (char == '-') {
            // Hyphen must be surrounded by letters
            if (i > 0 && i < N - 1) {
                val leftIsLetter = isLetter(i - 1)
                val rightIsLetter = isLetter(i + 1)
                return leftIsLetter && rightIsLetter
            }
            // Boundary hyphen check
            return false
        }

        // Any other character (space, punctuation, etc.) is a separator
        return false
    }

    // 2. Extract all words and count them
    val wordCounts = scala.collection.mutable.Map[String, Long]()
    var i = 0

    while (i < N) {
        if (isValidChar(i)) {
            // Start of a word segment
            var j = i
            // Consume characters as long as they are valid word characters
            while (j < N && isValidChar(j)) {
                j += 1
            }
            
            // The word is S[i...j-1]
            val word = S.substring(i, j)
            
            // Update count
            wordCounts(word) = wordCounts.getOrElse(word, 0L) + 1
            
            // Move index i to the position after the found word
            i = j
        } else {
            // Separator character, skip it
            i += 1
        }
    }

    // 3. Answer queries
    val ans = new Array[Long](queries.length)
    for (k <- 0 until queries.length) {
        val query = queries(k)
        ans(k) = wordCounts.getOrElse(query, 0L)
    }

    ans
}
